title: Mud Puppies – Young Children (Ages 6–8)

hunks.text: Have you ever heard of a MUD puppy? A mud puppy is not a dog at all! It is a real ANIMAL that lives in water. Mud puppies are a type of NEWT called a salamander. A salamander is a small, slippery CREATURE with four legs. Mud puppies are one of the most fun animals to learn about!

questions.question: A mud puppy is not a dog. It is an ______.
A mud puppy is a type of newt called a ______.
Name something true about a salamander from what we just read.
What animal do people walk on a leash?

hunks.imageAlt: A small lizard on a white surface

hunks.text: Mud puppies LIVE in lakes, rivers, and streams. They spend their whole LIFE in the water. They never come up onto LAND like some other salamanders do. Mud puppies love cold, DARK water where they can hide. They like to tuck under ROCKS and logs to stay safe.

questions.question: Mud puppies hide under rocks and ______.
Mud puppies love cold, dark ______.
Name a place where mud puppies live.
Pretend you are hiding under a big rock in a cold river. Make your body small and curl up tight!

hunks.imageAlt: Clear water flowing over textured rocks

hunks.text: One really cool thing about mud puppies is their GILLS. Gills are what they use to BREATHE underwater, like a fish. Most salamanders lose their gills when they grow up, but mud puppies KEEP theirs forever. Their gills look like soft, fluffy RED feathers on the sides of their head. Those feathery gills help them pull OXYGEN from the water.

questions.question: Mud puppies use gills to ______.
The gills look like fluffy, feathery ______.
Name something the text tells us about mud puppy gills.
Gills grow on the sides of a mud puppy's head. How many sides does a head have?

hunks.text: Mud puppies are BROWN or gray with dark spots on their SKIN. Their body is flat and wide, which helps them SWIM fast. They have four short legs with tiny TOES. Their tail is flat like a paddle and helps them STEER through the water. They look a little bit like a tiny dragon!

questions.question: A mud puppy's tail is flat like a ______.
Mud puppies are brown or gray with dark ______.
Name a body part the text tells us mud puppies have.
If you could pick any animal to look like, what would you choose?

hunks.text: Mud puppies come out at NIGHT to look for food. This means they are NOCTURNAL, which means active at night. They eat worms, BUGS, small fish, and crayfish. A crayfish is a small WATER creature that looks like a tiny lobster. Mud puppies are sneaky HUNTERS who creep slowly to catch their meals.

questions.question: Mud puppies come out at night, so they are ______.
Mud puppies are sneaky ______.
Name something a mud puppy likes to eat.
What do we call animals that only come out at night?

hunks.text: Mud puppies lay their EGGS in the spring under rocks or logs. The mother mud puppy GUARDS her eggs until they hatch. Baby mud puppies are called LARVAE, which means tiny babies that are still growing. The larvae are very SMALL when they first hatch. It takes about five years for a mud puppy to fully GROW up!

questions.question: Baby mud puppies are called ______.
The mother mud puppy ______ her eggs.
Name something the text tells us about mud puppy eggs.
questions.answer: LAID IN SPRING / UNDER ROCKS / UNDER LOGS

questions.question: How many years does it take a mud puppy to fully grow up?

hunks.imageAlt: A frog with inflated vocal sacs.

hunks.text: Mud puppies are IMPORTANT animals for rivers and lakes. They help CLEAN the water by eating things that could hurt the river. Scientists use mud puppies to check if a RIVER is healthy. If mud puppies are living there, the water is CLEAN and safe. They are like little water HELPERS for the whole ecosystem!

questions.question: Scientists use mud puppies to check if a river is ______.
Mud puppies help clean the ______.
Name something the text says mud puppies do that helps rivers and lakes.
questions.answer: CLEAN THE WATER / EAT THINGS / SHOW WATER IS SAFE

questions.question: Pretend you are a mud puppy swimming in a clean river. Move your arms like fins and swim around the room!

hunks.imageAlt: A clear river flows through a lush green forest.

hunks.text: Mud puppies need our HELP to stay safe. Dirty water and TRASH in rivers can make mud puppies sick. People can help by keeping rivers CLEAN and not putting harmful things in the water. Mud puppies have lived on Earth for a very long TIME. They are amazing little animals that deserve our CARE and respect!

questions.question: Dirty water and trash can make mud puppies ______.
Mud puppies deserve our care and ______.
Name something people can do to help mud puppies.
questions.answer: KEEP RIVERS CLEAN / NO TRASH / NO HARMFUL THINGS

questions.question: What is one thing you could do to help keep water clean?

hunks.imageAlt: Plastic bottle floating on murky water

citations: Harding, J.H. (1997). Amphibians and Reptiles of the Great Lakes Region. University of Michigan Press.
Petranka, J.W. (1998). Salamanders of the United States and Canada. Smithsonian Institution Press.
